source.title: The period of susceptibility to the physiological effects of unilateral eye closure in kittens.

confidence_rationale: The canonical demonstration that a competitive developmental window exists, is sharply time-bounded, and that deprivation within it produces near-permanent loss while the same deprivation outside it produces none. This is direct causal evidence for the 'permanent suppression without a plasticity window' half of INV-074. Discounted because it is single-system biology (visual cortex ocular dominance), so the universal-invariant scope rests on convergence with the ANN evidence (Achille 2019), not on this paper alone.
failure_signatures: Recovery after a 3-month in-window deprivation was very limited even with up to 5 years of subsequent eye opening -- consistent with INV-074's 'permanently suppressed regardless of diversity-signal magnitude', but it also means the claim's irreversibility is strong: a late diversity intervention cannot rescue a missed window.

Behavioural recovery required closing the formerly-normal eye (forcing competitive rebalancing); passive re-exposure was insufficient -- so the competitive-imbalance mechanism is necessary, not incidental, and any REE analogue that lacked active competition between pathways would not reproduce this.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Susceptibility to monocular deprivation in kitten striate cortex begins abruptly near the start of week 4, stays high to weeks 6-8, then vanishes by ~3 months; 3-4 days of closure within the peak window collapses the deprived eye's cortical influence (cells driven from ~85% to ~7%), whereas year-long closure in an adult produces no detectable effect; post-window recovery is minimal.
mapping.ree_translation: Hubel & Wiesel instantiate the biological half of INV-074: a model-building system under competitive (Hebbian-equivalent) dynamics permanently loses the under-driven pathway's representation unless competitive balance is established within a sharply time-bounded plasticity window. This is the empirical prototype of REE's claim that diversity circuits (ARC-065) must reach competitive weight before winner-take-all dynamics close the option space.
mapping.mapping_caveat: Ocular-dominance plasticity is one sensory system; INV-074 is a universal invariant. This entry evidences the necessity and time-boundedness of the window in biology only -- the substrate-independent scope requires the artificial-network convergence (Achille et al. 2019). The mapping from 'deprived eye' to 'minority diversity circuit' is an analogy of competitive dynamics, not an anatomical identity.
mapping.source_context: Kittens; lid-suture monocular deprivation at varied ages; single-unit recordings in striate cortex; lateral geniculate Nissl histology.

confidence_components.notes: Foundational, Nobel-grade causal developmental neuroscience -- source quality near ceiling. Mapping fidelity high for the window/permanence logic, lower for the 'monostrategy under Hebbian learning' framing which is REE's interpretation. Transfer risk elevated by single-sensory-system -> universal-invariant generalisation; mitigated by Achille 2019 convergence in the same folder.
